meta.title: H247 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every Mercedes GLA H247 engine — M282, M260, M139, OM654. Real UK costs, honest reliability data, and the 48V mild-hybrid explained. Get free quotes from 25+ vetted Mercedes specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes GLA-Class H247 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned H247 engine typically costs £1,500–£11,000 supply only, depending on engine — the M282 1.3-litre sits at £1,500–£3,200, the M260 2.0-litre at £2,800–£7,000, the OM654 diesel at £2,800–£5,000, and the M139 AMG engine at £5,000–£11,000. Fitting adds £520–£1,300 depending on engine complexity.

mainEntity.name: Which Mercedes GLA-Class H247 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The OM654.920 diesel (GLA 200 d/GLA 220 d) is our top recommendation — it's a modern, clean-sheet design that offers excellent fuel economy and proven reliability. The M260 2.0-litre petrol is also a solid choice, with well-understood maintenance requirements. The M282 1.3-litre is reliable but works hard in a heavy SUV.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes GLA-Class H247?

acceptedAnswer.text: AdBlue system failures on the OM654 diesel, turbocharger wear on the M282 1.3-litre petrol, cooling system stress on the high-performance M139 AMG engine, and 48V mild-hybrid system issues on post-2023 facelift models. The 8G-DCT transmission is generally reliable but requires regular servicing.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes GLA-Class H247 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, it's a solid modern Mercedes. The OM654 diesel is well-proven and reliable. The M260 2.0-litre petrol is also dependable. The M282 1.3-litre is reliable but works hard, so maintenance is key. The M139 AMG engine is robust but high-performance, so expect higher maintenance costs. The 48V mild-hybrid system on facelift models is promising but adds complexity.

mainEntity.name: Which Mercedes GLA-Class H247 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: No engine is an outright 'avoid', but we'd counsel caution on pre-facelift M282 1.3-litre petrols (GLA 180/GLA 200) without documented turbocharger service history — they can suffer from turbo wear. A neglected AMG GLA 45 or 45 S without cooling system history is also a risk unless you have a serious budget for maintenance.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my H247 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: AdBlue warning lights or loss of power (OM654), whining noise from the engine bay or loss of boost (M282 turbo), overheating or coolant loss (M139 AMG), or 48V warning lights (facelift models with EQ Boost) are the key warning signs. Check engine lights on any variant should be diagnosed immediately.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes GLA-Class H247 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Against a well-kept £18,000 H247, a £2,800–£5,000 OM654 recon is 15–30% — well within our 70% Mercedes-Benz threshold. Even the £5,000–£11,000 M139 AMG recon against a £35,000–£55,000 GLA 45 S is just 15–30% — a viable spend. These are modern, valuable cars; replacement almost always makes financial sense.

mainEntity.name: How long do Mercedes GLA-Class H247 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The OM654 diesel is proven to exceed 200,000 miles on well-maintained examples — AdBlue system maintenance is the key to longevity. The M260 2.0-litre petrol can reach 150,000+ miles with proper servicing. The M282 1.3-litre is too new for definitive data, but early signs are positive. The M139 AMG engine is robust but high-performance; expect 100,000+ miles with diligent maintenance.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the H247 and the X156?

acceptedAnswer.text: The H247 (2020–present, this page) is the second-generation GLA, built on the MFA2 platform with improved interior space, technology, and safety. The X156 (2014–2020) is the first-generation GLA, built on the older MFA platform with less interior space and older technology. Engines are mostly different; the H247 uses newer M282, M260, M139 and OM654 engines.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes GLA-Class H247 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate under the bonnet and in the vehicle documentation; block stamping is visible from below on most engines. Tell us your variant (e.g., GLA 200 d) and approximate registration year (pre- or post-2023 facelift) and we'll identify it — 25+ vetted Mercedes specialists will confirm it for free.

hero.h1: Mercedes GLA-Class H247 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the second-generation GLA crossover — from the Renault-sourced M282 1.3-litre petrol to the hand-built M139 in the AMG GLA 45 S. Real UK replacement costs, honest reliability data, and the 48V mild-hybrid system that defines the facelift. Backed by 520+ real UK OM654 enquiries in 2025 [MB-VERIFIED].

engineDatabase.h2: Mercedes GLA-Class H247 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the H247 crossover — including the 2023 facelift that introduced 48V mild-hybrid assistance to all petrol engines.

confidenceScore.title: Confidence Score
confidenceScore.text: OM654: 85% confidence — 520 [MB-VERIFIED] enquiries in 2025; well-documented engine. M260: 75% confidence — pattern-data tier; well-understood 2.0-litre turbo. M139: 75% confidence — pattern-data tier; AMG hand-built engine with proven durability. M282: 70% confidence — pattern-data tier; Renault-sourced engine with emerging reliability data. Hybrid variants: 65% confidence — pattern-data tier; additional complexity from electric systems.

overview.intro: The H247 is the second generation of the Mercedes-Benz GLA, introduced in 2020 as the successor to the X156. It shares the MFA2 platform with the A-Class and GLB, offering a taller, more practical body with improved interior space and technology. A 2023 facelift brought 48V mild-hybrid assistance to all petrol engines, along with updated MBUX infotainment. The range spans from the Renault-sourced 1.3-litre M282 petrol four-cylinder to the hand-built M139 in the 421 bhp AMG GLA 45 S.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: Mercedes-Benz MFA2 (Modular Front Architecture) • Predecessor: GLA-Class X156 • Successor: — (in production) • Sibling body styles in this generation: A-Class (W177), CLA-Class (C118), GLB-Class (X247).
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The OM654 diesel engine family generated 520 real UK enquiries in 2025 [MB-VERIFIED], concentrated on the GLA 200 d and 220 d as these compact SUVs continue to serve fleet and private buyers alike.

items.quote: The 2.0-litre four-cylinder diesel offers genuine performance (7.3s to 60mph) with excellent fuel economy and the reliability of Mercedes' modern common-rail system — AdBlue system maintenance is its only real demand.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want diesel torque and economy without the older OM651's issues.

items.quote: The 2.0-litre turbo four is the sweet spot of the petrol range — punchy, responsive, and without the M139's high-strung complexity. It's the engine that makes the GLA feel genuinely quick.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want performance without the AMG premium.

items.quote: The Renault-sourced 1.3-litre turbo is a clever engine, but its small displacement means it works hard in a 1.5-tonne SUV — turbocharger and timing chain wear are emerging as concerns on higher-mileage examples.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who prioritise running costs and accept the smaller engine's limitations.

items.quote: The hand-built 2.0-litre turbo delivers 421 bhp — the most powerful four-cylinder in production — but high-strung engines come with high-strung costs. Turbo and cooling system failures are expensive, and specialist labour is essential.
items.whoItsFor: AMG enthusiasts with the budget to maintain the most powerful four-cylinder ever made.

items.quote: The entry-level diesel offers genuine economy (50+ mpg) with the same core engine as the more powerful 220 d — and significantly lower purchase prices. It's the sensible choice for high-mileage drivers.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want diesel economy without the V6 or AMG premium.

items.quote: Often overshadowed by the 45 S, the GLA 35 offers genuine AMG performance (306 bhp) with significantly lower running costs than its big brother — and the 48V mild-hybrid system adds a useful 13 bhp boost.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want AMG credentials without the 45 S's running costs.

rows.ownershipVerdict: Entry Petrol — frugal but works hard.
AMG Entry — performance with manageable costs.
AMG Premium — most powerful, most costly.
Diesel Performance — more power, same risks.
Hybrid Risk — two powertrains, two risks.

economicsRule.title: The H247 rule
economicsRule.text: A £2,800–£5,000 OM654 recon against a £15,000–£30,000 H247 sits at 10–30% of the car's value — well within our 70% Mercedes-Benz threshold. Even the £5,000–£11,000 M139 V8-equivalent four-cylinder recon against a £35,000–£55,000 AMG GLA 45 S is just 15–30% — a viable spend on a well-maintained example. These are modern, valuable cars; replacement almost always makes financial sense.

keyTakeaways.question: Which H247 Costs Least To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: GLA 200 d (OM654) — cheapest diesel recon, best fuel economy.

keyTakeaways.question: Which H247 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: AMG GLA 45 S — M139 replacement and specialist labour costs.

keyTakeaways.question: Which H247 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: GLA 250 (M260) — petrol performance without the AMG premium.

problems.title: OM654 AdBlue System Failures
problems.affectedModels: H247 GLA 180 d, GLA 200 d, GLA 220 d (all OM654.920 variants).
problems.rootCause: The AdBlue (DEF) system uses NOx sensors and a heater that can fail — leading to warning lights, reduced power, and in some cases, the car refusing to start if the AdBlue system is not repaired.
problems.recommendation: On any OM654-powered H247 over 60,000 miles, have the AdBlue system inspected annually — a £500–£1,200 sensor replacement is cheap insurance against a £3,000+ engine replacement.

cta.label: Compare OM654 engine prices for your H247 GLA 200 d →

problems.title: M282 1.3-Litre Turbocharger Wear
problems.rootCause: The small-displacement turbo engine works hard to move a 1.5-tonne SUV — turbocharger bearings can wear prematurely, leading to whining noises, loss of boost, and in extreme cases, turbo failure.
problems.recommendation: If your GLA 180 or 200 develops a whining noise from the engine bay, have the turbo inspected immediately — a £500–£1,200 repair is far cheaper than the £2,500+ replacement that follows if the turbo fails catastrophically.

cta.label: Compare M282 engine prices for your H247 GLA 200 →

problems.title: M139 High-Performance Cooling System Stress
problems.affectedModels: H247 AMG GLA 45, GLA 45 S (all M139.980 variants).
problems.rootCause: The most powerful four-cylinder in production generates immense heat — the cooling system works at its limit, with water pumps, thermostats and coolant hoses all prone to premature failure.
problems.recommendation: On any AMG GLA 45 or 45 S, treat the cooling system as a consumable — an £800–£1,500 water pump replacement every 40,000 miles is cheap insurance against a £6,000+ engine replacement.

cta.label: Compare M139 engine prices for your H247 AMG GLA 45 S →

problems.title: 48V Mild-Hybrid System Failures (Facelift Models)
problems.affectedModels: H247 GLA 180, GLA 200, GLA 250, AMG GLA 35 (post-2023 facelift with 48V EQ Boost).
problems.rootCause: The 48V integrated starter-generator (ISG) and auxiliary battery are stressed by the system's frequent start-stop and boost operations — failures lead to warning lights, reduced power, and in some cases, complete system shutdown.
problems.recommendation: The 48V system is the defining risk of the facelift H247 — a £800–£1,500 preventative battery replacement at 50,000 miles is cheap insurance against a £3,000+ full engine replacement.

cta.label: Compare H247 engine prices with 48V system inspection →

replacementCosts.h2: H247 Engine Replacement Costs — By Engine and Condition
replacementCosts.note: No Generation variance from the baseline figures — the H247 shares its MFA2 platform with the A-Class and GLB, and engine-bay access is consistent across the range.

coreVariants.scopeNote: All variants listed are H247 SUV only (2020–present). The previous-generation X156 (2014–2020) is covered on its own Generation Page. Note that the GLA 250 e and 250 de are plug-in hybrids; the 250 e combines the 1.3-litre petrol engine with an electric motor, while the 250 de pairs the 2.0-litre diesel with electric drive.
